dependencies: Limited number of certified microelectronics component suppliers
Single supplier dependencies for certain components
Reliance on U.S. Government-approved supply sources for certain materials and components including microelectronic components
risks: Component shortages from vendors due to global pandemic, natural disasters, or shifting regulatory landscape
Supply chain disruptions from material availability and supplier performance
Extended lead times, pricing volatility, inflationary pressures and labor issues
Difficulties in timely procurement of necessary materials and components including microelectronics
Geopolitical conditions including sanctions and trade restrictive activities
Some materials and components previously sourced from areas now under sanctions (specialty metals from Russia, certain equipment from China)
Supplier financial and operational difficulties outside direct control

regulatory_risks: U.S. Government budget deficit and national debt affecting spending priorities
Congressional appropriations and administrative allotment of funds uncertainty
Continuing Resolution (CR) or government shutdown impacts
Changes in U.S. Government spending priorities with new Administration
Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) compliance requirements
Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement compliance
International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) compliance
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) compliance
Export control laws and regulations
U.S. anti-boycott legislation
Product certification or stewardship requirements
Sourcing restrictions and tariffs
Cybersecurity regulations
Environmental standards including greenhouse gas emission limitations
Cost Accounting Standards (CAS) requirements
Procurement integrity requirements
Competition law compliance
Information security and data privacy regulations
Suspension or debarment risks from U.S. Government contracting
Loss of export privileges
Environmental laws (Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act, Resource Conservation Recovery Act)
Climate-related disclosure requirements (SEC Release Nos. 33-11275 and 34-99678)
Debt ceiling requirements
Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023 (FRA) caps and automatic sequestration

sanction_risks: Economic and trade sanctions including tariffs
Sanctions on Russia affecting specialty metals sourcing
Sanctions on China affecting equipment sourcing
Risk of sanctions or trade restrictive actions on current sourcing areas
International boycotts and U.S. anti-boycott legislation
